channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Musk says 'civilization will disappear' unless birth rates change
item.description: "The birth rate is very low in almost every country, and unless that changes, civilization will disappear," Musk said in an interview with Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: 1,002 people reported dead in Myanmar's earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Myanmar's powerful 7.7 magnitude earthquake has killed 1,002 people, as the death toll keeps climbing amid rescue efforts. The military government said Saturday another 2,376 people were injured, with 30 others missing, in addition to the dead.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Close call reported between Delta flight and Air Force jet near Reagan National
item.description: A Delta Air Lines flight had a reported close call Friday with a U.S. Air Force jet near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Jay Blackman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Feds on alert for Tesla 'Global Day of Action' after nationwide violence leads to arrests
item.description: A law enforcement expert shared how American residents can assist police in alerting and preventing violence during the Tesla Takedown Global Day of Action this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Transgender teens fight back against Trump's ban on participation in girls' sports
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — Parker Tirrell, 16, enjoys her art classes, scrolling on TikTok and working at her new job at a pet store. But most of all, the transgender teen loves playing soccer. Until last year, that wasn’t a problem.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New wave of smaller, cheaper nuclear reactors sends US states racing to attract the industry
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — With the promise of newer, cheaper nuclear power on the horizon, U.S. states are vying to position themselves to build and supply the industry's next generation as policymakers consider expanding subsidies and paving over regulatory obstacles.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia University's interim president steps down and returns to former post
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University’s interim president Katrina Armstrong has resigned, returning to her post running the New York school’s medical center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 4 dead after severe storms in the South Texas-Mexico border region and hundreds rescued
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — Drenching rains along the Texas-Mexico border trapped hundreds of people in flooded homes and in cars stranded in high waters, scrambling rescue crews to calls for help that continued Friday even as the downpours let up. At least four people died, including some who drowned.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ and JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Oregon city at heart of high court homelessness ruling blocked from enforcing camping ban
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — An Oregon judge issued a preliminary injunction Friday blocking the city at the heart of a U.S. Supreme Court ruling on homeless encampments from enforcing its camping rules unless it meets certain conditions, as part of a lawsuit filed by advocates.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stolen truck pursuit ends with violent, multi-vehicle pileup in Southern California
item.description: One of the more dangerous and destructive police pursuits in recent memory unfolded on live television in south Los Angeles County Friday afternoon, involving a driver in a white stolen pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Texas reaches 400 measles cases as US deals with outbreaks in 5 states
item.description: At least five states have active measles outbreaks as of Friday, and Texas' is the largest with 400 cases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Voice of America wins in court, for now, as judge blocks Trump administration from firing staff
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Voice of America can’t be silenced just yet. A federal judge on Friday halted the Trump administration’s efforts to dismantle the eight-decade-old U.S. government-funded international news service, calling the move a “classic case of arbitrary and capricious decision making.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk says xAI buys social media platform X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday his artificial intelligence firm xAI has bought his social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard revokes security clearances, access to classified information for Biden, Harris, Clinton, others
item.description: Several of President Donald Trump's political opponents had their security clearances revoked, said Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Authorities find skeletal remains at home of Georgia man who died in Hawaii
item.description: Investigators at the medical examiner's office do not suspect any foul play, a representative told Nexstar.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sevier County student hit while waiting for bus
item.description: SEVIER COUNTY, Tenn. (WATE) — A 10-year-old Sevier County Schools student was taken to the hospital after they were struck while waiting for the bus Friday morning, according to a school system spokesperson.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: The top-rated seafood restaurant in every state, according to Yelp
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Top-rated seafood restaurant in every state: Report
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: This is the top-rated seafood restaurant in California, according to Yelp
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner. Knowing this, the analysts at Yelp have sought to identify the top-rated seafood restaurants in each state.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump: House should take up Senate-passed DC funding fix immediately
item.description: President Trump is urging swift passage of legislation to fix what members have called a “mistake” by Congress that forced significant cuts to Washington, D.C.’s local budget. Trump on Friday wrote on Truth Social that the House should “immediately” take up the “D.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows Bangkok high-rise collapse during magnitude 7.7 earthquake
item.description: A devastating earthquake struck Myanmar and neighboring Thailand on Friday, resulting in the deaths of at least 150 people, primarily in Myanmar. The quake caused the collapse of a bridge and a dam.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump takes aim at perceived wokeness at museums, federal sites
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday signed an executive order seeking to exert more control over what is displayed at Smithsonian museums and federal sites, citing what the White House called “divisive narratives” promoted by certain museums.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KTLA 5

item.title: Juror in 1st Karen Read trial joins defense, says case shook faith in criminal justice system
item.description: A Massachusetts attorney who served as an alternate juror in the mistrial of Karen Read last year has joined her defense in her second murder trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawyer for Michigan couple held in Mexican prison says resort company is at fault
item.description: A Michigan couple remains behind bars in a Mexican prison, accused of breaching a timeshare contract — but their lawyer said the resort that filed the criminal complaint against them breached the contract first.
item.dc:creator: Shajaka Shelton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A powerful new app for card collectors
item.description: To a card collector, there’s nothing quite like ripping open a fresh pack. “You find someone you like and you collect them… and I think it’s special,” started Jay Moslehi, also known as MOJO SPORTS on YouTube.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump administration moves to end union rights for many federal workers
item.description: The order targets agencies it says have a national security mission but many of the departments don’t have a strict national security connection.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The US is hunting for eggs in Europe
item.description: It is common in parts of Europe for consumers to buy eggs that still have feathers and chicken poop stuck to them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Americans’ outlook on economy becomes a bit more pessimistic: Poll
item.description: Around 57 percent of those earning under $50,000 said their financial situation is “bad,” while 39 percent argued it is “good,” the poll found.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Trump shifts approach to Greenland takeover
item.description: What looked like a Trump administration charm offensive in its gambit to take over Greenland, billed as a cultural heritage tour led by Second Lady Usha Vance, has turned into something different with the inclusion of her husband and the explosion of a national security scandal at home.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KTLA 5

item.title: What we know about the deadly air crash between a passenger jet and a US Army helicopter
item.description: A string of crashes this year along with a near miss have brought attention to air travel, which remains overwhelmingly safe.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Civil rights groups say push to dismantle Education Department will undo hard-won gains
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The rights of Americans to self-determine how to educate their children — a hotly contested matter that stretches back to at least the Civil War — have long been intertwined with the principle of equally and equitably educating children across racial lines.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Toddler sneaks onto White House grounds
item.description: A toddler slipped through the White House fence on Wednesday, the Secret Service said in a post on the social platform X. “Just after 6:30 p.m. on Wednesday, U.S. Secret Service Uniformed Division officers observed a child slip through the White House north fence.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge: High heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional” but did not order the state to begin installing air conditioners.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Trump shifts Greenland approach amid blowback, Signal scandal
item.description: Planned trips this week to Greenland’s capital, Nuuk, and a dogsledding race were canceled in favor of a more limited trip to visit American military installations on the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5
